test_cases.title: Verify that a user can successfully log time with valid inputs
test_cases.evidence_quote: User can successfully log time with valid inputs
preconditions: User must be allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Select a valid date, work log category, a project, enter hours greater than zero, and provide a work description
5. Save the entry as Draft or submit it
expected_result: The system should accept the inputs and allow the user to save or submit the time log entry successfully.

test_cases.title: Ensure that invalid or missing inputs block submission
test_cases.evidence_quote: Invalid or missing inputs block submission.
preconditions: User must be allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Leave one or more required fields empty or enter invalid data (e.g., negative hours, future date)
5. Attempt to save or submit the entry
expected_result: The system should prevent submission and display an error message for each invalid or missing input.

test_cases.title: Validate that the date picker is required and must be a valid date
test_cases.evidence_quote: Date | Date Picker | Required; Must be a valid date; Cannot be a future date | "Date is required" / "Please enter a valid date"
preconditions: User must be allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Leave the date field empty and attempt to save or submit the entry
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the date is required.

test_cases.title: Validate that work description field must be available
test_cases.evidence_quote: Work Description | Text Area (String) | Required; Min 10 chars; Max 500 chars | "Description is required" / "Minimum 10 characters required"
preconditions: User must be allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Leave the work description field empty and attempt to save or submit the entry
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the description is required.

test_cases.title: Ensure that projects dropdown is required and must select one project from the list
test_cases.evidence_quote: Projects | Dropdown (Enum) | Required; Must select one project from the list (Project category same as project creation process) | "Please select a project"
preconditions: User must be allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Leave the projects dropdown empty and attempt to save or submit the entry
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that a project must be selected.

test_cases.title: Ensure that archived projects cannot be selectable
test_cases.evidence_quote:
Exceptions
If project is archived, it must not be selectable
preconditions: User must be allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Attempt to select an archived project
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the selected project is archived and cannot be selected.

test_cases.title: Ensure that hours must be numeric and greater than zero
test_cases.evidence_quote: Hours must be numeric and greater than zero
preconditions: User is allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen and click 'Add work Log'
3. Select a date in the past month
4. Choose a valid work log category from the dropdown
5. Select a project from the list
6. Enter non-numeric characters or zero for hours
7. Enter a valid work description
expected_result: The system should display an error message: 'Time must be greater than 0'

test_cases.title: Validate that the date picker is required and must not select a future date
test_cases.evidence_quote: Required; Must be a valid date; Cannot be a future date
preconditions: User is allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen and click 'Add work Log'
3. Select a date in the future month
4. Choose a valid work log category from the dropdown
5. Select a project from the list
6. Enter valid hours (e.g., 1h 0m)
7. Enter a valid work description
expected_result: The system should display an error message: 'Please enter a valid date'

test_cases.title: Ensure that the system allows users to select any previous date but not beyond the current month.
test_cases.evidence_quote: The system must allow users to create daily time log entries and can select any previous date but not beyond the current month.
preconditions: User is allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Select a date that is within the current month but in the past
5. Fill out all required fields with valid data
expected_result: The system should allow the user to select the previous date and proceed to save or submit the entry.

test_cases.title: Ensure that the system blocks submission if any required field is missing.
preconditions: User is allocated or permitted to log time on the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application
2. Navigate to the role-based home screen
3. Click 'Add work Log' button from the landing page
4. Fill out all fields except one (e.g., Work Description)
5. Attempt to save or submit the entry
expected_result: The system should block submission and display an error message indicating that a required field is missing.
